text: A project has been initiated in the organization to build new substation. During project planning, the project manager has a meeting with the procurement manager to discuss what are the goods and services that the project might need from outside the organization. In this meeting what should the project manager provide?
explanation: The main goal for any procurement in a project is to transfer risks from buyer to seller. So the project manager must provide an understanding for project risks to ensure that contract terms and conditions transfer all risks from buyer to seller. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 463 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 74–76

choices.text: A procurement management plan and a list of recommended contract types.
Nothing, make-or-buy decision will be done during project executing.
An understanding of the project risks.
A list of recommended potential sellers.

text: Sam is managing a large IT project. Some work of this project will be outsourced to other companies. To solicit proposals from prospective sellers, all of the following documents should be shared with the potential sellers, except:
explanation: For large procurements, the procuring organization may elect to either prepare its own independent estimate or have a cost estimate prepared by an outside professional estimator to serve as a benchmark on proposed responses. It is used internally in the organization and can't be shared with the potential sellers. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 479

choices.text: Procurement statement of work
Request for Proposal (RFP)
Policies of the buyer's organization that may affect the presented sellers' proposals
Independent cost estimates

text: Dennis is a project manager for a four phases sequential industrial project. He has scheduled a meeting with concerned stakeholders to hand-off phase product and to evaluate the project performance compared to the project and business documents. This activity can be referred to all the following terms except:
explanation: Phase gates may be referred to by other terms such as, phase review, stage gate, kill point, and phase entrance or phase exit. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 21

choices.text: Stage gate
Phase review
Kill point
Transition phase

text: You’re managing a construction project when your customer required to finish the project earlier than planned due to external circumstances. A team member heard that in a situation like this, you can use schedule compression by either crashing or fast-tracking the schedule, but he’s not sure which one to use. What do you tell him?
explanation: Schedule compression will shorten the project time either by crashing which will increase the cost or by fast tracking that will increase project risk because critical path activities that were originally planned sequentially will be implemented in parallel. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Crashing the project adds risk, while fast-tracking adds cost.
When you crash a project, it always shortens the total duration of the project.
Crashing the project adds cost, while fast-tracking adds risk.
When you fast-track a project, it always shortens the total duration of the project.

text: A risk with high detectability means that:
explanation: Detectability is the ease with which the results of the risk occurring, or being about to occur, can be detected and recognized. Where the risk occurrence can be detected easily, detectability is high. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 424

choices.text: Risk urgency can be detected easily.
Risk manageability can be detected easily.
Risk probability can be detected easily.
Risk occurrence can be detected easily.

text: Phillip is managing a research and development project. The scope of the project is not clear, he decided to outsource the project work to another company. He agreed with the procurement manager to use the cost plus incentive fee (CPIF) contract. All of the following are true about this type of contract except:
explanation: In cost reimbursable contracts the total cost of the contractual work is unknown at the beginning of the work, as the scope of work is not precisely defined at the beginning. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 472 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: The seller receives all costs of the performed contractual work, in addition to a predetermined incentive fee based upon achieving certain performance objectives.
If the final costs are less or greater than the original estimated costs, then both the buyer and seller share costs from the departures based upon a prengotiated cost-sharing formula.
This type of contracts should be used if the scope of work is expected to change significantly during the execution of the contract.
The total cost of the contract is known and agreed upon before starting the work.

text: You are managing a large IT project. Midway through the project, you learn that new equipment is needed to support project delivery. The procurement specialist has informed you that there will be a delay in the delivery of the needed equipment due to custom clearance and shipping lead time which will negatively impact your project timeline. What should you do next?
explanation: First things first. There is a delay risk which will have a negative impact on the project. So, a risk should be identified and added to the risk register and inform stakeholders about the probable delay. . Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: Communicate this risk to the project stakeholders and add an entry in the risk register.
Search for local suppliers to provide the needed equipment.
Escalate to senior management and ask for support.
Raise a change request to extend the project timeline.

text: An external customer has requested from XYZ organization to develop a new mobile application. This customer has worked three times before with the organization on three different previous projects. He is known that his requirements evolve throughout the project and might end up with a totally different product than which was originally required. In light of this, which of the following approaches will most fit this project?
explanation: When requirements are uncertain, volatile, or complex and are expected to evolve throughout the project, a more adaptive approach may be a better fit. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 39

choices.text: Waterfall
It is not relevant.
Adaptive
There is not enough information to decide.

text: Adam is managing an industrial project when one of the project team members informed him that the dimensions of one of the project deliverables are different than the planned dimensions and thus it will be unable to operate with the machine. Adam asked the team member to determine the basic underlying reasons that cause this variance and also to determine whether these reasons might cause other variances in other project deliverables. Which tool should the team member use in this situation?
explanation: Root cause analysis is an analytical technique used to determine the basic underlying reason that causes a variance, defect, or risk. A root cause may underlie more than one variance, defect, or risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 292 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 177

choices.text: Audits
Alternatives analysis
Root cause analysis (RCA)
Process analysis

text: Which of the following is a minimum viable product (MVP)?
explanation: A mobile application without user interface and a computer without operating system are unusable products. A pencil that has eraser and cushion grip has features more than the essential needed features for a pencil. They are all unlikely to be MVP. A chair that has no back is a MVP because it has the minimum feature that it can be used to sit on. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 61

choices.text: A pencil with eraser and cushion grip.
A mobile application that has no user interface.
A computer without an operating system.
A chair that has no back.

text: A project manager on a hardware design project using plan-driven approach has decided to incorporate some agile principles and tools to his project. He asks the team to use iteration demos. What would be a likely result if the team uses iteration demos?
explanation: As the team completes the features usually in the form of user stories, the team periodically demonstrates the working product. The stakeholders sees the demonstration and accepts or declines stories. As the stakeholders will provide feedback on a product increment not the whole product which prevents the team from heading in a wrong direction. So the cost of change will be much less, because it will be a modification to a small piece of work which has been produced during the iteration. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Less number of defects in the product increment
Validate scope less frequently
Reduced cost of change
Less number of change requests

text: Two team members have a conflict on the type of material to be used in a specific activity. The conflict is not significant and is not escalated to the project manager. Which of the following is the best course of action for the project manager?
explanation: The parties to the conflict are primarily responsible for resolving the conflict. If they cannot resolve the conflict, it must be escalated to the project manager. If the dispute is escalated to a higher level, it must be escalated to the human resources department. If the dispute is technical, the situation must be escalated to the functional manager. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition

choices.text: Try to find a compromise that brings some degree of satisfaction to both parties.
Involve their functional managers as it is a technical conflict.
Allow the team members to resolve the conflict themselves.
Have a meeting with both team members and try to find a solution using a direct and collaborative approach.

text: You are in the middle of executing a major new construction project. The project is very important and highly visible in your organization. The sponsor informed you that he is worried that the project will not meet the quality standards it was intended to. If the project doesn't meet the quality standard, the customer will not be satisfied. What should you do in this situation?
explanation: Assuring the sponsor that the project would meet the quality standards is not a solution. Historical information of your past projects don't indicate the status of the current project. The Quality Management Plan doesn't indicate the current situation. Forming a quality assurance team will indicate if the team is following the correct processes and procedures which meets the quality standards and metrics. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 294

choices.text: Assure the sponsor that during the Plan Quality Management process, it was determined that the project would meet the quality standards.
Form a quality assurance team.
Show him the historical information of your past projects that they all met the quality standards.
Check the results from the last quality management plan.

text: Smith is a project manager for a large software implementation project. The project includes a large number of stakeholders. He is an experienced project manager and knows that stakeholder engagement is a key factor to project success. After identifying all stakeholders, he needs to categorize them according to their power, interest and influence. Which of the following categorization models is the most appropriate to use?
explanation: Stakeholder cube combines power, interest and influence which already exist in power/ interest and power/influence grids into a three-dimensional model that can be useful to project managers and teams in identifying and engaging their stakeholder community. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 513

choices.text: Power/interest grid
Power/influence grid
Power/interest and power/ influence grids
Stakeholder cube

text: An activity has an early start (ES) of day 2, and a late finish (LF) of day 18. The activity duration is 5 days. Which of the following statements is true regarding this activity?
explanation: Activity duration = Early finish – Early start = Late finish – Late start. Activity float = Late finish – Early finish = Late start – Early start. Activity Float = LF – EF = LS – ES. Activity Duration = LF – LS = EF – ES. 5 = EF – 2. EF = 2 + 5 = 7. Activity Float = LF – EF = 18 – 7 = 11. This means that activity has float, so activity is not on the critical path. The project float doesn't equal the activity float. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 210 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: The activity is on the critical path.
The project float is 11 days.
The project will be delayed.
The activity is not on the critical path.

text: During a hardware design project, a critical path activity was delayed because the predecessor activity was completed later than planned. After investigating the cause of the problem, it was found that the team member responsible for executing the predecessor activity was late in completing the activity, because the estimated time for completing the activity was shorter than the actual needed time. This most probably happened because:
explanation: The team member didn't participate in estimation of durations, thus the estimate is not accurate. The inputs for the estimates of duration originate from the person or group on the project team who is most familiar with the nature of the work in the specific activity. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 196 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 55-58

choices.text: The team member was not involved in estimating the activity duration.
The resources assigned to perform the activities were less than the actual needed resources.
The project manager was not involved in estimating the activity duration.
Historical information was not used properly in estimating activity duration.

text: The project manager says to the team member: "You need to listen to me because I have been put in charge. Perform the work assigned to you". Which power is the project manager using?
explanation: The project manager here is using the power derived from his position (formal or legitimate). Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 63

choices.text: Legitimate
Situational
Charismatic
Punitive

text: A project team is working on his first project using adaptive approach. The team facilitator is coaching them about using Kanban board and the importance of limiting work in progress (WIP). All of the following are advantages of limiting WIP on agile projects, except:
explanation: Work in progress (WIP) is the term given to work that has been started but has not yet been completed. Excessive levels of WIP are associated with a number of problems like hiding bottlenecks, representing risk in the form of potential rework, and consuming investment capital and delivering no return. It is not related to managing project resources and their utilization. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 66

choices.text: Limiting WIP helps agile teams to avoid the risk of potential rework.
Limiting WIP helps agile teams to easily identify and remove bottlenecks.
Limiting WIP allows agile teams to optimize throughput.
Limiting WIP allows agile teams to properly manage project resources and manage their utilization to work more efficiently.

text: While testing the stability of the electronic board used in your project, you discover that it becomes unstable when it works in high temperature which doesn’t meet your company’s quality standards. You are sure that it will function as it is with the customer, and you don’t think the board needs to meet the quality level specified. What should you do?
explanation: Stating in your reports that the board simply “meets our quality needs” or changing the quality standards to meet the achieved level are against the professional and social responsibility of the project manager. Documenting the problem in lessons learned register maybe done later because it doesn't solve the current problem. So the best solution is to report the lesser quality and try to find a solution. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 299

choices.text: State in your reports that the board simply “meets our quality needs”.
Change the quality standards to meet the achieved level.
Document this problem in lessons learned register and send this board to customer.
Report the lesser quality and try to find a solution.

text: What should the scrum master do in the daily standups?
explanation: In a daily standup, the scrum master's role is to listen and record any obstacles to the team's progress for immediate follow up. The team is self organized and the discussion is run by the team for the team, so there is no need for facilitator or someone to resolve their conflicts. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 53 and 54

choices.text: Facilitate and control the meeting.
Ask the team about the root cause of their problems.
Resolve team conflict.
Listen and note any problems for follow-up.

text: Karim has been hired as a team member in a large construction project. His native language is Arabic and the organization's language is English. He is having trouble understanding the emails received every day. To overcome this problem, he installed an instant translation software on his phone to translate any received email to Arabic to be able to understand these emails. What did he do?
explanation: Decoding means that the data received is translated by the receiver back into a form useful to the receiver. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

choices.text: Decoding
Encoding
Feedback
Acknowledge

text: All of the following are stakeholders except:
explanation: A stakeholder may affect or be affected by project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 9

choices.text: The bank from which you get a loan to fund your project
A financial expert at your company’s competitor
A risk management expert in your organization who will help you in evaluating risks on your project
The user of the product of the project

text: A project team in a maintenance center is working on repairing computers, but they are having difficulty creating a project charter. What is the best description of the problem?
explanation: The work mentioned here is not a project at all. Maintenance work is operational, ongoing and repetitive work, there is no need to create a project charter. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 16

choices.text: They haven’t identified project objectives.
They haven’t identified the product of the project.
The end date has not been set.
They are working on a process and not on a project.

text: Amit is managing a large telecommunication project to install a new mobile network in four cities. Part of the project work is outsourced to a contractor. Midway through the project, the contractor has submitted an invoice for additional work. Amit reviewed the invoice and found that the work billed is already involved in the contracted scope. What should Amit do?
explanation: The project manager needs first to understand the reason behind the extra billed amount. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 494

choices.text: Ask the procurement department to deal with this issue.
Submit a change request to include the extra billed amount.
Meet with the contractor to clarify the reason behind the additional work and review the project scope.
He should reject the invoice.

text: Thomas is managing a large project to produce a new pharmaceutical drug. The project is very important for his company and quality has a top priority. Strict quality standards have been put in place and they must be followed. In order to achieve the required quality standard, Thomas approved on a business trip for two team members to have training on the new quality standards. The cost of team members training is an example of:
explanation: Prevention costs are costs related to the prevention of poor quality in the products, deliverables, or services of the specific project. Examples on prevention costs are training and equipment. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 282 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 88

choices.text: Failure costs
Cost of nonconformance
Appraisal cost
Prevention cost

text: Lisa is managing a construction project. The project is to build a new office building and it is very important to the customer. The project team has just calculated the critical path duration and informed the customer that the project will take one year. The customer informed Lisa that the project duration is too long and the building should be opened with an official ceremony after ten months. What should she do?
explanation: Adding resources to some critical path activities will shorten the project schedule, this practice is known as crashing. Fast tracking the project can't be done if the project risk is high, because fast tracking increases project risk. Escalating the issue to the project sponsor is not a proactive solution. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Fast track the critical path activities if overall project risk is high.
Add resources to some critical path activities to adjust the project end date to meet the imposed date by the customer.
Inform the customer that the critical path is the minimum project duration and it has no flexibility.
Escalate the issue and ask for guidance from the project sponsor.

text: Two team members are working on an agile project in two different countries. As per the communication management plan, they should hold a daily standup through video conference. How can the agile coach best monitor the effectiveness of their communication and coordination?
explanation: As per agile Manifesto that working product is the primary measure of progress. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Periodically ask team members if the product components are fitting together as planned.
Listen in on the daily standups once a week to monitor the conversations.
Meet with the two team leaders every week to check on their progress.
Trust that the plan is working and rely on the team leaders to notify him of any problems.

text: The planning of your project is complete. As a project manager you are about to conduct the kick-off meeting with all stakeholders and you feel that the strategic directions for the project need to be presented first. Who is the best person to present it in the kick-off meeting?
explanation: The Sponsor is who presents the strategic directions for the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 86 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 179

choices.text: The Project Manager
The Sponsor
The Customer
The project management team

text: The project team has identified a top risk that ranked higher than the previously identified risks. They cannot identify a suitable response to the new identified risk, which risk response strategy would they apply?
explanation: No risk response strategy was selected, this means that the risk is accepted. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Mitigation
Escalation
Acceptance
Transference

text: Helena is a team member who has recently joined a running construction project. She noticed that there is a difference between leadership styles of Kevin, the project manager of the current project and Andrea the project manager of her previous project. Kevin always focuses on goals and achievements, while Andrea focuses on team members learning, development and relationships. What leadership styles do they adopt?
explanation: Transactional style focuses on goals, feedback, and accomplishment, while servant leader style focuses on other people’s growth, learning, development, autonomy, and well-being; concentrates on relationships, community and collaboration. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 65 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 41

choices.text: Kevin adopts transactional style, while Andrea adopts charismatic style.
Kevin adopts transformational style, while Andrea adopts servant leader style.
Kevin adopts transactional style, while Andrea adopts servant leader style.
Kevin adopts laissez-faire style, while Andrea adopts interactive style.

text: An advantage of the burnup chart is that:
explanation: The burnup chart is useful because it can show changes in scope, making the impact of those changes visible. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 63

choices.text: It can show the remaining work to be done.
It can show changes in project costs.
It can show changes in scope.
It can show the individual team members performance.

text: Antony is managing an industrial project. He has received a poor quality deliverable from one of the suppliers. He requested the supplier to fix the defects he found. Antony wants to ensure that no defects are found in the future deliveries from the supplier. What should he do?
explanation: Contracts require formality, as there is a contract with the supplier, the project manager must ensure first that the quality requirements exist in the contract and then he should ask for an evidence that the requirements are being followed through quality assurance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 468

choices.text: Review the contract requirements with the supplier and request quality assurance evidence.
Don’t pay for the defective deliverables and pay only for the accepted ones.
Ask the procurement department to address this issue with the supplier.
Hire a team of inspectors to inspect the deliverables from this supplier.

text: Which of the following represents a lose-lose negotiation?
explanation: Compromise entails that all parties will not be satisfied 100% and each has to give up something. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168-169

choices.text: The project manager decides to give up on his own approach and use the approach provided by Mark in order to gain confidence.
The technical team negotiates different approaches and they will implement Phillip's approach rather than the approach provided by Mary.
The project manager has agreed with the two team members who are in a conflict to compromise.
The supplier succeeded in negotiation with the procurement manager to reach a fair and reasonable price for the service which he will provide.

text: You are leading an agile team working on a software project to develop a mobile application. You and the team have completed the last iteration and are going to demonstrate the completed product increment to the stakeholders during the sprint review. What is the most valuable feedback of the product demo?
explanation: "All the required features exist in the increment" means that the objectives are met and product increment is accepted. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: All the required features exist in the increment.
The customer representative wants to thanks the team for their efforts.
The customer may assign future projects to your team.
The marketing manager liked the product increment and wants to show it to the customer.

text: You are managing a project to install a new intra-network for a customer. The project work has been completed and the deliverables were handed over to the customer. You have closed all open claims, shared the final project report, updated the lessons learned repository, closed all procurements and obtained a final acceptance from the customer for the project deliverables. What should you do next?
explanation: Activities needed to transfer the project's products, services, or results to the next phase or production and/or operations is one aspect of project closure. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Transfer the intra-network to operations.
Celebrate with the team.
Inform the suppliers officially that the project has been completed.
Inform functional managers that the resources assigned to the project are now available to be assigned to any other project.

text: Fatima is a scrum master for a software project. During the first sprint, she notices that some developers are not engaged during the daily scrum. The developers usually don't participate and sometimes they check their cell phones during the daily scrum. How can she address this issue?
explanation: The role of the scrum master is to help everyone understand scrum theory and practice. She should coach the team about the importance of the daily scrum and the importance of their participation. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: Instruct the team about the importance of the daily scrum.
Decrease the frequency of the daily scrum to be twice per week instead of once per day.
Bring this issue as an impediment during the next daily scrum.
Discuss the issue in the next retrospective.

text: Which of the following statements best describes the team charter?
explanation: The team charter is a document that establishes the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines for the team. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 192

choices.text: The team charter is a document that contains the project objectives, high level project plan and success criteria.
The team charter is a document that contains the KPI's and appraisals of the project team members.
The team charter is a document that establishes the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines for the team.
The team charter is a document that contains the roles and responsibilities of the project team members.

text: You have just been authorized as a project manager for a small project, identified project stakeholders and are moving into project planning. A project stakeholder asks you for the project’s budget and cost baseline. What should you tell him?
explanation: The project budget can be found in the project charter but it is not a detailed one. It is possible to complete the estimate before creating the project management plan because the ROM (Rough Order of Magnitude) estimate is created during the Initiation process. Even small projects must have a budget, so the best choice is that the project budget and baseline will not be finalized and accepted until the planning processes are completed. Domain: Process. Refer to PMBOK 6th edition, page 254 and PMBOK 7th edition, page 62.

choices.text: The project budget and baseline will not be finalized and accepted until the planning processes are completed.
It is impossible to complete an estimate before the project management plan is created.
The project budget can be found in the project charter, which has just been completed.
The project management plan will not contain the project’s budget and baseline; it is a small project.

text: The organization's CEO is highly concerned about one of the agile projects being implemented in his organization. He wants to know the progress of the project. What is the best demonstration of progress that the team can come up with?
explanation: The second value of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software over comprehensive documentation. The seventh principle also states that Working software is the primary measure of progress. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Burndown Chart
Working product
Information Radiators
Project Status Report

text: To determine how accurate and well understood the risk information on the project is, it must be analyzed. And also you need to determine whether more research is needed before a qualitative assessment can be done. It is most important to complete which of the following?
explanation: Risk data quality assessment evaluates the degree to which the data about individual project risks is accurate and reliable as a basis for qualitative risk analysis. The use of low-quality risk data may lead to a qualitative risk analysis that is of little use to the project. Monte Carlo analysis, sensitivity analysis and decision tree analysis are from the tools and techniques of the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 423

choices.text: Decision tree analysis
Sensitivity analysis
Monte Carlo analysis
Data quality assessment

text: Alex is a project manager working for automobile manufacturer for a project to build more fuel-efficient, high stability small cars. He and his team determine how to inspect the product of the project to meet the stakeholders’ needs and expectations, as well as to meet the goal for the product’s performance and reliability. In which process are they involved and which tool are they using?
explanation: During the planning phase in the Plan Quality Management process, the project manager and the project team determine how to test or inspect the product, deliverable, or service to meet the stakeholders’ needs and expectations, as well as how to meet the goal for the product’s performance and reliability. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 285

choices.text: Manage Quality, Process Analysis
Manage Quality, Audits
Control Quality, Inspection
Plan Quality Management, Test and inspection planning

text: Which of the following best describes the difference between waterfall and adaptive life cycles?
explanation: The term plan-driven or waterfall is used to describe the emphasis on an upfront plan and then execution of that plan. Adaptive life cycles are agile, iterative, or incremental. The detailed scope is defined and approved before the start of an iteration. Adaptive life cycles are also referred to as agile or change-driven life cycles. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 17 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 35

choices.text: Projects that adopt waterfall life cycles use co-located teams while projects that adopt adaptive life cycles use virtual teams.
Projects that adopt waterfall life cycles produce tangible products while projects that adopt adaptive life cycles produce services or results.
Waterfall is plan-driven, while adaptive is change-driven.
They are the same.

text: Diego has just joined a construction project as a design team leader instead of Roberto who has resigned from the organization. The project management plan has been already completed and the project team are currently executing the project work. Diego wants to know the roles and responsibilities of his team members in details. Which of the following techniques is used to provide detailed description of team members responsibilities?
explanation: Team member responsibilities that require detailed descriptions can be specified in text oriented formats. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 317

choices.text: Resource Breakdown Structure
Responsibility Assignment Matrix
RACI chart
Text-oriented formats

text: A project manager wants to follow Herzberg's theory of motivation, all of the following are motivating agents that she should follow except:
explanation: Salary is a hygiene factor. Poor hygiene factors may destroy motivation, however improving hygiene factors may not improve motivation. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 158

choices.text: Recognition
Responsibility
Self-actualization
Salary

text: Chris and Anne are two key stakeholders in a software project. They expressed their concern to the project manager that they are not updated with project changes. During last week change control board approved two change requests to the scope baseline and they were not updated. The project manager apologized to them and solved the problem by sending an automated notification email from the organization’s change control system whenever a change request is approved to the concerned stakeholders. Which document should be updated accordingly?
explanation: The communications management plan is a component of the project management plan that describes how project communications will be planned, structured, implemented, and monitored for effectiveness. It includes stakeholder communication requirements, information to be communicated and person or groups who will receive the information. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 79

choices.text: Stakeholder engagement plan
Communication management plan
Stakeholder register
Change management plan

text: A software project has been initiated in an adaptive environment. The team decided to build the demo first and add features to the initial product. This is known as:
explanation: Continuous Delivery is the practice of delivering feature increments immediately to customers, often through the use of small batches of work and automation technology. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 151

choices.text: Conduct a spike
Test-Driven Development (TDD)
Behavior-Driven Development
Continuous Delivery

text: A team member informs the project manager that he has added an extra functionality to the work package assigned to him which was not included in the design. The new functionality did not add any extra cost and he created it during his weekend. The team member believes that it will satisfy the customer and he will appreciate it. What should the project manager do?
explanation: Adding unplanned functionality is referred to as gold plating and it is not recommended. The team should build what was designed only. Domain = Process.

choices.text: Reward the team member for meeting and exceeding customer expectations.
Remove the new functionality and instruct the team member to build only what is included in the design.
Thanks the team member for his effort and invite the customer for a meeting to inform him about the new added functionality.
Submit a change request to add the new functionality to design.

text: You are a project manager for a new construction project. Your project is to build a new housing compound which contains 10,000 houses. The houses are substantially similar. What must you do?
explanation: If the project has some repeated activities, the lessons learned of the first few installations can be used to improve the process on the remaining installations. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 289

choices.text: Plan in continuous improvement activities.
Make sure the first and last of these work packages is scheduled close in time to each other.
See what can be done to remove the similarity.
Make sure the same resource completes all the similar work packages.

text: You are a project manager for a large tele-communication project that was initially budgeted at $9,600,000. While preparing for your weekly status report you find that you have spent till now $4,000,000, the cost variance for your project is $-500,000, the schedule performance index is 1.12. In light of this, what is the earned value of the project?
explanation: CV=EV-AC, EV=AC+CV Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

text: A team member has moved from a team that adopts traditional approach for their projects to another team that adopts agile approach. He has difficulties to understand what incremental delivery means. What should his team leader tell him?
explanation: With incremental delivery, the team regularly deploys working increments of the product over the course of the project. So it is an opportunity to start realizing the benefits of the product and get an early return on investment. Incremental delivery reduces the amount of rework by finding issues earlier and thereby contributing to the delivery of value on the project. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 57

choices.text: The team regularly deploys non-functional increments of the product over the course of the project.
The team regularly improves agile processes over the course of the project.
The team regularly improves the quality of the product over the course of the project.
The team regularly deploys functional increments of the product over the course of the project.

text: Linda is managing a cyber security project for ABC company. During project executing, the customer representative has been changed. Few weeks later, the new representative complains that he is not aware that the project has fallen behind schedule because he did not receive any project status reports since he has been assigned. However, all other stakeholders are aware of the status. Usually, Linda share the project status at the end of each week via email. How should she address this situation?
explanation: It is clear that the stakeholder register and the communication management plan were not updated when the new representative joined the project, because all other stakeholders are aware of the project status except him. So, the best answer is to update the stakeholder register and the communication management plan with the new representative information and ask him if he has any other communication requirements. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 389, 508 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: Update the stakeholder register and communication management plan and discuss the project requirements with the new representative.
Apologize to the new representative and send him the project status report.
Share the communication management plan with the new representative.
Update the list of recipients of the project status report email.

text: All of the following statements about bidder conference are true except:
explanation: In bidder conference you must meet all prospective sellers together at once, not each one alone. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 487 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 70

choices.text: The buyer should meet each potential seller separately.
Bidder conferences are held prior to proposal submittal.
Bidder conferences are also called contractor conferences, vendor conferences, and pre-bid conferences.
All sellers receive the same procurement documents.

text: You have been promoted to a project manager position and assigned to manage a project that involves multiple stakeholders from five countries. You are being mentored by a senior project manager who has a great experience of managing large scale and multinational projects within your organization. He advised you to pay more attention to evaluating the engagement levels of the identified stakeholders. Which of the following documents contains specific strategies for engaging stakeholders in your project?
explanation: The stakeholder engagement plan may include specific strategies or approaches for engaging with individuals or groups of stakeholders. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 522 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 187

choices.text: Stakeholder Management Plan
Stakeholder Register
Stakeholder Engagement Plan
Communication Management Plan

text: You are working for a major pipeline project. The project is very important and highly visible for your organization. It is performed using 32 human resources. As you review the project results, you notice that the value of the work accomplished is $1,200,000 at the last reporting point. You know the schedule performance is good, since your SPI is 1.2 while you have spent $1,050,000 to this point, what are the CPI, and the schedule and cost variances?
explanation: EV = 1200000, SPI = 1.2, AC = 1050000, SPI = EV/PV, PV = EV/SPI = 1200000/1.2, PV = 1000000 CPI = EV/AC = 1200000/1050000 = 1.14 SV = EV-PV = 1200000 – 1000000 = 200000 CV = EV-AC = 1200000 – 1050000 = 150000 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

text: A risk owner is allocated to each individual project risk as part of which risk management process?
explanation: A risk owner, who will be responsible for planning an appropriate risk response and for reporting progress on managing the risk, will be allocated to each individual project risk as part of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 426

choices.text: Identify Risks
Plan Risk Responses
Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis
Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis

text: Michael is a project manager for a product development project. He and his team members are holding a weekly project status meeting. A team member asked the project manager that they will need additional resources to perform a critical path activity which has been delayed. The project manager told him that he forgot to do something before adding resources. What might the team member forget to do?
explanation: They should first try to look for options to compress the schedule before asking for additional resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Look for options with the customer before meeting with the project manager.
Level the resources.
Remove float in near critical path.
To try to compress the project schedule before asking for additional resources.

text: Which of the following is not true about projects and systems thinking?
explanation: Systems thinking entails taking a holistic view of how project parts interact with each other and with external systems. A project might work within other larger systems like programs or portfolios and may have subsystems that are required to integrate effectively to deliver the intended outcome. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 37

choices.text: Systems thinking entails taking a holistic view of how project parts interact with each other and with external systems.
A project might work within other larger systems, and a project deliverable may become part of a larger system to realize benefits.
A project may have subsystems that are required to integrate effectively to deliver the intended outcome.
A project might either work within other larger systems or might have subsystems, can't work with both at the same time.

text: You have just been promoted to a project manager position. You are assigned to manage your first project. You want to report your project schedule. You asked a more experienced project manager about the best method to report your schedule. He told you there are several methods to report your schedule like network diagrams, milestone chart and Gantt chart. Under which circumstances is it best to use a network diagram rather than a Gantt chart?
explanation: In this question you can read only the last sentence without reading the rest of question. A network diagram is used to show interdependencies between activities. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 218 and PMBOK 7th edition, Pages 58–60

choices.text: Gantt chart is always the best way to report project schedule.
To show interdependencies between activities.
To track progress or to report to the team.
To report to the sponsor.

text: You are a project manager for electronic boards manufacturing project. Your team has identified a risk with some of the electronic components you are using on your project. Some of these electronic components may be defected, based on past projects, you've figured out that there's a high probability that about 18% of the electronic components may be faulty. You decide to buy an extra 20% of the electronic components so that you will be prepared for those defects and your project won't be delayed. Which response strategy are you using?
explanation: By purchasing extra components, you mitigate the risk. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Avoid
Accept
Mitigate
Transfer

text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto principles is more related to value driven delivery?
explanation: The reason projects are undertaken is to generate business value. This definition of progress as “working systems” or "working software" creates a results-oriented view of the project and encourages the team to have value-driven delivery as their focus throughout the project.

choices.text: The best architectures, requirements, and designs emerge from self-organizing teams.
Simplicity—the art of maximizing the amount of work not done—is essential.
Build projects around motivated individuals. Give them the environment and support they need, and trust them to get the job done.
Working software is the primary measure of progress.

text: Your sponsor wants to know the Estimate at Completion for your project. The project was budgeted at $30,000, and to date you have spent $20,000 but only completed $10,000 worth of work. You are sure that the future work will be accomplished at the planned rate.
explanation: As the work will be accomplished at the planned rate EAC=AC+BAC-EV= 20000 + 30000 - 10000 = 40000. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 265 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: Midway through the project, the project team want to measure business value to ensure that the project deliverables are still aligned with the business case and benefits realization plan. Which of the following measures can the project team use?
explanation: Business value measurements are used to ensure the project deliverable stays aligned to the business case and the benefits realization plans. Metrics that measure financial business value include ROI, NPV, cost-benefit ratio and planned benefits delivery compared to actual benefits delivery. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 102

choices.text: TCPI
NPS
SPI
ROI

text: A project team adopts extreme programming method (XP) is working on a software project. XP applies the concept of pair programming where the team members are writing software code together as a pair and reviewing each other's work to determine if the code conforms to the requirements documentation or not. What will be a result of these reviews?
explanation: A goal of the Control Quality process is to determine the correctness of deliverables. The results of performing the Control Quality process are verified deliverables that become an input to the Validate Scope process for formalized acceptance. As the two developers inspect each other completed work, so they are doing the same as Control Quality process. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 102 and PMBOK 6th edition, Page 305

choices.text: Product Increment
Minimum Viable Product
Accepted Deliverables
Verified deliverable

text: You are a project manager of a software project when a previously unidentified risk is discovered. Your project is near completion and this risk may delay project completion date. What should you do next?
explanation: The risk here has been discovered, but hasn't occurred yet. So the best choice is to identify the risk and its impacts and add the risk to the risk register. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 411

choices.text: Assign a risk response owner to the discovered risk.
Reevaluate the Identify Risks process.
Create a workaround.
Qualify the risk.

text: You are managing an industrial project. The project team has successfully produced the first release of the project deliverables. The deliverables are being inspected by the quality control department in the organization. What should be done next?
explanation: Project deliverables are reviewed with the customer to gain formal acceptance of the product through the Validate Scope process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164

choices.text: Verified deliverables should be reviewed with the customer to gain formal acceptance of the product.
Verified deliverables should be delivered to the customer and begin project closure procedures.
Document lessons learned and begin project closure procedures.
Continue producing the remaining project deliverables.

text: Tony is a project manager for a complex software project. The project is very large and is very important to the customer. He has finished the software system, and obtained acceptance from customer, but few days later, the software system was found to give unexpected results. The customer is very angry and he needs a solution for this problem. Which of the following best describes what should he do first?
explanation: Reviewing the customer’s inspection process is not the responsibility of the project manager. He may apologize to the customer later when he knows the root cause of the problem. Doing nothing is against the professional and social responsibility of the project manager. So, the best choice is to review the validate scope process during which the product was accepted. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 164

choices.text: He should do nothing, the customer has accepted the project.
He should apologize to the customer.
He should review the validate scope process.
He should review the customer’s inspection process.

text: Tom is a project manager at training and consulting company. The company regularly builds teams to create products for clients and train them on those products. When the product is delivered, the team is dissolved and assigned to other projects. What kind of organization is he working for?
explanation: In project-oriented organizations, staff are released after project end and achieving project objectives. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Multi-divisional
Project-oriented
Functional
Strong matrix

text: You work for a construction firm which has a long history in construction projects. Your company is bidding on a contract with the government to build a new bridge. Which of the following contract types has the most risk for your company?
explanation: Here you work for the seller not for the buyer. The highest risks for the seller are in FP contracts. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 462 and 471 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: CPIF
T&M
FFP
CPAF

text: All of the following are included in the project communications management plan, except:
explanation: Contact details of each stakeholder are included in the stakeholder register. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and 514 and and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Guidelines and templates for project status meetings
The use of a project website and project management software
Contact details of each stakeholder
Resources allocated for communication activities, including time and budget

text: The project manager has just communicated the responsibility assignment matrix to the project team. A team member who has just been assigned to the project finds that he is involved with three team members in a procurement audit. He asks the project manager about the purpose of the procurement audit. The project manager told him that the purpose of the procurement audit is to:
explanation: Audits are a structured review of the procurement process. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 498 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 79

choices.text: Review the work being performed by the contractor.
Review the type of the selected contract.
Review the contract cost.
Review the procurement process.

text: You are a project manager for a product development project. You are now evaluating whether to buy or lease an equipment. The daily lease cost is $120, while the cost of purchasing is $7200 investment cost with $30 daily cost. How long will it take the lease cost to equal the purchase cost?
explanation: *All Lease Costs = 120 * D D is number of days All Buy Costs = 7200 + 30 * D 7200 + 30 *D = 120 D 7200 = 120 D – 30 D 7200 = 90 D D = 7200/90 D = 80 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 473 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 65

choices.text: 60 days
80 days
240 days
48 days

text: A project is being implemented using a predictive approach. There is a high turnover rate for the project team members. More then 50% of the project team members who were assigned at the beginning of the project have left the project and new team members replaced them. The project manager wants to adopt an adaptive approach to accommodate these changes. If his trial is successful, it can be applied on all projects within the organization. What should the project manager do first?
explanation: Before making any change, the project manager needs to evaluate the benefits and the risks for making this change and take the decision accordingly. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 160

choices.text: Ask the team members about their knowledge about agile methodologies.
Evaluate the impacts of moving to adaptive approach.
Schedule a training session for the team members about agile methodologies.
Ensure there is a sufficient budget to make this change.

text: Which of the following theories focuses on motivating employees by creating a work-life balance?
explanation: Theory Z focuses on motivating employees by creating a job for life where the focus is on the well-being of employees and their families. This style of management seeks to promote high productivity, morale, and satisfaction. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 160

choices.text: Theory X
Theory Y
Theory Z
Theory of needs

text: Midway through the project, one of the scheduled project activities could not be implemented as its scheduled time and resource contradicts with a regular maintenance activity. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Rescheduling the project activity without looking for possible options is not a good solution. The project manager should discuss all possible options first with functional manager. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 348 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Submit a change request to reschedule the project activity.
The project manager should inform the assigned resource to prioritize the project activity.
The project manager should discuss with the functional manager to check if the schedule can be adjusted.
The project manager should ask PMO to formally prioritize the project work over the operational work.

text: Mark is managing a large software project. The project is very large, it consists of 75 team members and is planned to take two years. At the end of each quarter, he performs an assessment for each team member to know the areas of strengths and weaknesses for each one. Which process is he involved in?
explanation: Individual and team assessment tools give the project manager and the project team insight into areas of strengths and weaknesses. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 342

choices.text: Manage Team
Acquire Resources
Develop Team
Control Resources

text: While monitoring the cost performance in a small project which was budgeted for $85,000 over a 9 weeks period. As of today, you’ve spent $42,000 of your budget to complete work that you originally expected would cost $44,000. According to your schedule, you should have spent $50,000 by this point. Based on this situation, what is the status of the project?
explanation: AC = 42000, EV= 44000, PV = 50000 SPI = EV/PV = 44000/50000 <1 behind schedule CPI = EV/AC = 44000/42000 >1 under budget Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: Under budget and ahead of schedule.
Over budget and ahead of schedule.
Under budget and behind schedule.
Over budget and behind schedule.

text: A project team member asks you about who is responsible to distribute the risk status to project stakeholders. Where should he look to know who is responsible for this task?
explanation: Communications Management Plan contains information to be communicated, including language, format, content, and level of detail; person responsible for communicating the information; and person or groups who will receive the information. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Communication management plan
Risk management plan
Risk register
Responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)

text: You have been assigned as a project manager for a large construction project to build a new housing compound. The project is very large and very important to your organization. You are planning the project with the project team when a key stakeholder suggests a different type of tree to be used for landscaping. The suggested type of tree is different from the one specified in the project scope. You and the project team agree that the suggested type is better and cheaper than the previous type as it will not affect the project schedule, but it will save $30,000 from project costs. You decide to use the new type of trees. What should you do first?
explanation: You are still planning your project, so the project is still not baselined. Before the baselines are established, changes are not required to be formally controlled by the Perform Integrated Change Control process. Impacts have already been evaluated as the new change will not affect the project schedule but it will decrease project costs. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Create a change request.
Evaluate impacts of using the new type.
Change the scope baseline and cost baseline to use the new type.
Consult the Change Control Board.

text: Monica is leading a project to develop a new electronic device in predictive environment. She discusses with the project team using a new component suggested by a team member to have better performance. The project team needs to test the viability of using the new component. They have a tight schedule and there is a risk if they decide to use the new component and it turns out later that it affects the product performance, the project will face delays and financial penalties. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Incorporating agile methods with traditional methods would make the overall project management approach hybrid. In this situation, the project team could use the agile technique of a spike. Spikes are useful for learning and may be used in circumstances such as: estimation, acceptance criteria definition, addressing a risk, and understanding the flow of a user’s action through the product. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 56

choices.text: Discuss this issue with the sponsor.
Hold a spike.
Plan for a contingency reserve.
Use the new component.

text: Catherine is a new project manager in a construction firm. She is attending a project management webinar about the importance of strategic and business management skills for a project manager. The trainer indicated many reasons for the project manager to be knowledgeable enough about the business, which of the following can't be one of them?
explanation: Technical project management skills not strategic and business management skills are needed by the project manager to be able to achieve project objectives like scope, time and cost. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 58

choices.text: To be able to explain to others the essential business aspects of a project.
To be able to work with the project sponsor, team, and subject matter experts to develop an appropriate project delivery strategy.
To be able to implement that strategy in a way that maximizes the business value of the project.
To be able to achieve project objectives like scope, time and cost.

text: Mark is a project manager on a software project. He is always watching his team members during their work, during their breaks, and when they leave. He always walks around the office to make sure that everyone is doing his work. He always makes all the project decisions. What kind of manager is he?
explanation: According to Mc Gregor theory, X manager is always watching his team members, doesn't trust them and believes that they may avoid work whenever possible. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 160

choices.text: Theory X
Theory Y
Motivating
Effective

text: A new product development project is currently being implemented using adaptive approach. The customer has agreed with the performing organization on delivery milestones to deliver specific features on each milestone. Midway through the project, the customer wants to show a tangible deliverable of the project with some specific features to his stakeholders as soon as possible which will require shifting some milestones to earlier dates. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: As per the agile manifesto values 3 and 4 that agile team value customer collaboration over contract negotiation and value responding to change over following a plan. So they should try to support the customer to reprioritize his requirements and try to deliver the features with highest priority to the customer as soon as possible. At the same time the team should check internally whether the customer requirements are achievable or not. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 8

choices.text: The team should discuss with the customer his priorities and the features he would like to be delivered early and discuss internally what can be delivered early.
The customer requirements will impact the delivery milestones and hence the contract. The team should ask the customer to raise a change request.
The team should escalate this issue to the project sponsor for decision making.
The team should assess the customer request and inform him that shifting any milestone will have serious implications.

text: You are a project manager of a software project in a predictive environment. The customer required a change to a major work package. After many meetings and discussions, finally, the project team members determined the specifications of the change. What should you do first?
explanation: After determining the specifications of the change, you must calculate the risks associated with the change (the impacts on other project constraints), then you can write the change request and submit it to the CCB for approval. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Calculate the risks associated with the change.
Make the change as its specifications are determined.
Look for other changes from the customer before making the change.
Send the change request to the change control board.

text: During project closure, a project manager performs activities related to satisfy completion of project or phase and activities related to the completion of contract or agreement. Which of the following are involved in both activities?
explanation: Confirmation that the product is accepted is involved in both activities which are related to satisfy completion of project or phase and activities related to the completion of contract or agreement. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Confirmation that the product is accepted
Finalizing open claims
Reassigning personnel
Dealing with excess project material

text: All of the following statements are true about project business case, except:
explanation: The business case may be used throughout the project life cycle. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 30

choices.text: The business case may be used only before and during the project initiation and may result in a go/no-go decision for the project.
The business case lists the objectives and reasons for project initiation.
The project business case helps measure the project success at the end of the project against the project objectives.
The project business case is used as a basis for the authorization of further project management activities.

text: Midway during a sprint, one of the developers determines that the scrum team will not be able to complete the items in the sprint backlog. Who should be present to review and adjust the product backlog accordingly?
explanation: During the sprint, scope may be clarified and re-negotiated between the product owner and developers as more is discovered. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: The product owner
The developers
The scrum team
The product owner and developers

text: During a project weekly meeting, the project manager displays the project status to the team members. The project SPI = 0.83 and CPI = 0.98. The project team are in a very critical situation, because the project is running behind schedule and there is only two months remaining for the project to be completed as per the contract with the customer. If the project is not delivered on time, they may be subjected to a penalty. He wants to clarify the critical situation, so he raised his voice and spoke loudly with an angry look on his face. What did he do?
explanation: Encoding means that the message is coded into symbols, such as text, sound or some other medium for transmission. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

choices.text: Decoding
Encoding
Feedback
Acknowledge

text: Which of the following is the best interpretation for the Agile Manifesto value "working software over comprehensive documentation"?
explanation: The second principle of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software over comprehensive documentation. This value reminds us to focus on the purpose or business value were trying to deliver, rather than paperwork. Agile documentation should be “barely sufficient”—just enough to cover our needs. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Creating a high quality software should be done before creating project documentation.
Creating a high quality software is the top priority, because creating a documentation without a working software has no value.
Software projects normally don't need documentation.
Creating a high quality software is a top priority and creating project documentation can be done if there is available time in each sprint.

text: You are managing a product design project. You are identifying risks with your team. You identified a risk that a supplier may not deliver material on time. You consult the lessons learned from previous projects and discover that the supplier has a history of providing material later than the due date. You evaluate the probability and impact of the risk, and find that if it occurs, it will cost the project $5,000 and two weeks delay. You decide to contract another supplier to get the needed material. What type of risks resulting from using the other supplier?
explanation: Secondary risks result from the selected risk response strategies. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 448 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 125

choices.text: Secondary risks
Residual risks
Accepted risks
Low priority risks

text: Documenting the questions and answers in a bidder conference and sending them to all bidders is an example of which kind of communication?
explanation: Anytime you have any communication having to do with bidding, it’s always formal written. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 485-487 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 70

choices.text: Informal verbal
Formal written
Formal verbal
Informal written

text: Adam is a manager in his organization, he is responsible for ensuring that maintenance work is running efficiently and smoothly. Gerard is also a manager in the same organization, he is responsible for providing management oversight for maintenance work in the organization. Stephen is also a manager in the same organization, he is responsible for leading his team to ensure that the new testing station is installed and working properly within predetermined schedule and budget. Which of the following statements best describes their roles?
explanation: The functional manager focuses on providing management oversight for a functional or business unit. Operations managers are responsible for ensuring that business operations are efficient. The project manager is the person assigned by the performing organization to lead the team that is responsible for achieving the project objectives. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 52

choices.text: Adam is a functional manager, Gerard is an operations manager and Stephen is a project manager.
Adam is an operations manager, Gerard is a functional manager and Stephen is a project manager.
Adam is a project manager, Gerard is a functional manager and Stephen is an operations manager.
Adam is an operations manager, Gerard is a project manager and Stephen is a functional manager.

text: During executing a major motor design project, two team members are having disagreement about choosing the type of a component that will be used in the project. The project manager has offered to help. The project manager suggests a compromise, and the team members want to solve the problem. Who should decide on the best course of action?
explanation: Conflict is best resolved by those involved in the conflict. The project manager should try to resolve conflicts as long as he has authority. If not, the sponsor or functional managers may be called to assist. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 348 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 168

choices.text: Customer
Team members
Project manager
Sponsor

text: Where should you document positive risks?
explanation: All risks are recorded in the risk register, there is nothing called positive risk register. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 417 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 185

choices.text: In the positive risk register
In the risk register
In the watchlist
In the Risk Management Plan

text: Sam has just taken over a project which is being executed using predictive approach. He has noticed that the project team members waste much time in responding to stakeholders questions and inquiries. Stakeholders usually interrupt the team members anywhere anytime which makes the team members unable to focus on project work. Which of the following should Sam do first?
explanation: Sam needs first to review the communications management plan to determine how stakeholder questions and inquiries should be managed. Then can judge whether the stakeholders are violating the plan or not. Then, he can make whatever changes that might be needed to shield the project team from wasting their time in responding to the stakeholders' questions. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 389 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: Ask the team to better manage their time and dedicate a slot to answer stakeholders questions and inquiries.
Ask the stakeholders to formally address their questions to him.
Ask the team members not to respond to any stakeholders inquiries as this is not their responsibility.
Review the communication management plan and submit change request if needed.

text: You are managing a project which was budgeted at 1,250,000$ and scheduled to be completed within six months. During project executing, the sponsor asked you about the status of the project. You collected the needed data to calculate earned value measurements. You found that the project is 35% complete although it should be 40% complete. You have already spent 550,000 $ to reach this point in the project. What would you tell the sponsor?
explanation: EV is the value of work accomplished. EV = 0.35 * 1,250,000 = 437,500. PV is the value of the planned work = 0.4 * 1,250,000 = 500,000. and AC is the actual incurred cost AC= 550,000. SPI = EV/PV= 0.875. CPI = EV/AC = 0.8. So the project is behind schedule and over budget Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project is ahead of schedule and over budget.
The project is ahead of schedule and under budget.
The project is behind schedule and over budget.
The project is behind schedule and under budget.

text: You are the project manager of an industrial project. Your organization signed a contract with a supplier to provide your project with 15 computers as per your requirements. After one month the procurement manager called you to inform you that the shipment you requested has arrived containing 18 computers. What should you do?
explanation: A contract should clearly state the deliverables and results expected, including any knowledge transfer from the seller to the buyer. Anything not in the contract cannot be legally enforced. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461

choices.text: Issue a change order to change the contract specifications.
Accept the shipment.
Expect to receive a claim from the seller.
Send the shipment back.

text: A project has been initiated in ABC organization to develop a new electronic device. The project entails high uncertainty because this is the first time to develop this device. Which of the following is the best course of action?
explanation: Sometimes uncertainty can be reduced by finding out more information, such as conducting research, engaging experts, or performing a market analysis. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 119

choices.text: The project team should use well-designed experiments to reduce the amount of uncertainty.
The project team should use waterfall development approach, because it entails extensive risk management processes.
The project team should gather more information by conducting market analysis.
The project team should plan for enough contingency reserve to accommodate budget overruns.

text: A new project manager has just been assigned a construction project that is ongoing and is three weeks behind schedule. The team members are about to be distributed to another new project based on the original time estimates by the functional manager. What type of organization is the new project manager in?
explanation: There is a little authority for a project manager in a centralized (functional) organization. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 47

choices.text: Hybrid
Balanced matrix
Centralized
Project-oriented

text: A project team has been formed to work on a product development project. It is the first time for this team to work together. After few weeks, while the team members figure out how to work together, some conflict and struggle happened between the team members. Which of the following is the best leadership style to lead a team in this stage of the project?
explanation: As the team passes into the storming phase, there will generally be plenty of disagreement, open conflict, and harsh dialogue. During this stage, the leader needs to assume the coaching style to help team members resolve conflicts without damaging relationships. So the coaching leadership style should provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition.

choices.text: Provide a more directive than supportive leadership.
Provide a more supportive than directive leadership.
Provide high levels of both directive and supportive leadership.
Provide low levels of both directive and supportive leadership.

text: Which of the following documents are most subjected to reviews and approvals?
explanation: Although all project documents may be subject to some form of review and approval, the legally binding nature of a contract means it will be subjected to a more extensive approval process, often involving the legal department. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 191

choices.text: Project charter
Project management plan
Contracts
Benefits management plan

text: What is the best method to report to the project team the status of the project schedule during project executing?
explanation: Bar Charts are the best method to report to project team, as they determine what has been achieved in the project. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 217

choices.text: Milestone chart
Network diagram
Activity list
Bar chart

text: Albert is a project manager for a large project to develop electronic tickets for railroad stations instead of paper tickets. He is being mentored by Jim, an experienced project manager.
