Albert is discussing with Jim that the project is very large and he needs to delgate some work to specialists. Jim advised him that he can delgate some work to specialists but the accountability of _____ can't be delegated or transferred.
explanation: The accountability of Integration Management can't be delegated or transferred. The project manager is the one who combines the results of other Knowledge Areas and has the overall view of the project. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 72

choices.text: Schedule Management
Procurement Management
Integration Management
Quality Management

text: Midway through the project, three team members have resigned from the organization. This may delay the activities assigned to them. This risk was already addressed during project planning. No action has been taken after their resignation. What should the project manager do?
explanation: The project manager should review the risk register as the risk was already addressed, so there should be a risk response. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 417 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 185

choices.text: Schedule a meeting with the project team to plan for risk response.
Visit the risk register.
Contact the HR department and ask for support.
Calculate the duration of activities assigned to them and submit a change request to extend the schedule by this duration.

text: Which of the following statements best describes an "issue"?
explanation: A negative project risk that has occurred is considered an issue. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 397 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 53

choices.text: A project risk that has occurred.
A negative project risk that has occurred.
A negative risk that may occur.
A positive risk that has occurred.

text: Phil has been assigned to manage a new network installation project. The project team is formed from highly competent and engaged staff within the organization. Which of the following is the best leadership style to use in this situation?
explanation: For environments with highly competent and engaged staff, empowered delegation elicits more productivity than centralized coordination. Domain = People. The Standard for Project Management, Page 41

choices.text: Laissez-faire
Directive
Empowered delegation
Autocratic

text: You are leading a project team for the fourth project they work on together. As the team members have worked together before on many projects, consensus and cooperation have been well-established and the team is mature, organized, and well-functioning. What is the most appropriate leadership style you can use?
explanation: Performing teams are autonomous, empowered, self-managing, and self-policing. They require little more than to be pointed in the right direction and given regular recognition and appreciation for their high performance. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 30

choices.text: Directing
Supporting
Coaching
Delegating

text: Vicky through an instant messenger software. Michael is worried that Vicky might misunderstand him and think that he is angry. So, he added a smiley emotion to clarify that he is not angry. What did Michael do?
explanation: Encoding means that the message is coded into symbols, such as text, sound or some other medium for transmission. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

choices.text: Decoding
Encoding
Feedback
Acknowledge

text: A medical devices manufacturer is adopting agile methodology in their current projects. A project team is working to develop a medical product that require a lot of documentation. What should they do?
explanation: It is true that the second value of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software (product) over comprehensive documentation. However, some highly regulated industries, such as medical devices, require alot of documentation—that’s just part of the work that needs to be done. Which means that the required documentation is a project deliverable, it is a part of the product of the project. This value refers to the kind of documentation that isn’t included in getting the work done properly. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 8

choices.text: Create the needed documentation.
Create the medical product and if they have available time create the needed documentation.
Consult the project sponsor.
Follow the Agile Manifesto values and give more attention to a working product over documentation.

text: During planning your project, you are balancing project constraints scope, time, cost, quality,..The customer required that quality of this project must be as high as possible. This type of projects is relatively new to your organization. What is the best thing to do in this situation?
explanation: Benchmarking is not effective here because this type of projects is new. Using Ishikawa diagram or control charts are not proactive solutions. So the best answer is to audit work as it is being completed to look for any needed improvements. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 294

choices.text: Audit work as it is being completed to look for any needed improvements.
Use Ishikawa diagram when there is a defect.
Benchmark the current project quality standards to identify best practices.
Use control charts to make sure that the process is in control.

text: A petroleum company is investing in a series of initiatives to look for petroleum in many countries so that the company can be competitive in 5 years. The initiatives are tracked and managed together because this goal is very important to the success of the company. This is an example of:
explanation: A portfolio is a group of projects, programs and operational work which have a common strategic goal and here is one startegc goal which is that the company becomes competitive in 5 years. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 11 and The Standard for Project Management, Page 4

choices.text: A portfolio
A program
A project
An enterprise environmental factor

text: Which of the following is an example of the agile principle "Continuous attention to technical excellence and good design enhances agility"?
explanation: Refactoring is the housekeeping, cleanup, and simplifications that need to be made to code to ensure it is stable and can be maintained over the long term. It is an example of technical excellence. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 121

choices.text: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
Refactoring
Value prioritization
Limiting WIP

text: You are managing a project with a schedule performance index (SPI) of 1.03 and a cost performance index (CPI) of 0.96. How would you best describe this project?
explanation: SPI>1 means ahead of schedule, CPI<1 over budget Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project is ahead of schedule and within its budget.
The project is behind schedule and within its budget.
The project is ahead of schedule and over its budget.
The project is behind schedule and over its budget.

text: Remon is managing a project to design more fuel-efficient cars. During product inspection, it was found that there is leakage in freon system. The leakage was due to installing a screw larger than the correct one. Remon submitted a change request to solve this problem. The change control board approved on changing the defected parts with other ones to conform with the planned requirements. This is known as:
explanation: Defect repair is an intentional activity to modify a nonconforming product or product component. And in this situation the long screw caused the product to be nonconforming to the planned requirements. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 96

choices.text: Corrective action
Preventive action
Defect repair
Updates

text: Chris has been assigned to manage a large construction project which involves a large number of stakeholders. Chris has started with the project team to collect project requirements from stakeholders. It seems that many stakeholders have contradicting requirements and some stakeholders have unclear requirements. What should Chris and his team do in this situation?
explanation: Requirements should be collected from all stakeholders. Filling a survey will neither uncover all requirements nor clear conflicts between contradicting requirements. The best option is to hold face-to-face meetings with all stakeholders to understand their requirements and expectations. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 140 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 82

choices.text: To effectively collect the exact needed requirements, the project team needs to collect requirements from key stakeholders only.
To remove the conflict between contradicting requirements, the project team needs to use Delphi technique as a form of expert judgment.
To build consensus regarding the project requirements, the project team needs to hold face-to-face meetings with all stakeholders to understand their requirements and expectations.
To collect requirements from a large number of stakeholders, the project team needs to send a survey template to be filled out by all stakeholders.

text: Nicole is a project manager for a major software project. The project is very important for the customer. She and her team have finalized the project management plan which resulted in a schedule that is too long. She needs to compress the schedule when she analyzed the network diagram, she found that it can't change. She has extra personnel resources. What is the best thing to do?
explanation: Resource leveling will increase the project duration, Monte Carlo analysis doesn’t relate to increasing or decreasing project durations, fast tracking can’t be applied in this case as she can’t change the network diagram, thus the activities that were originally planned sequentially can’t be changed to be in parallel, crashing the project is the best answer because she has extra available human resources. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 215 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 58-60

choices.text: Perform Monte Carlo analysis
Level the resources
Crash the project
Fast track the project

text: Chris has been assigned to manage a large software project using virtual team distributed in four countries. There are many internal and external stakeholders involved in this project. At the beginning of the project, Chris and the project team are developing a stakeholder engagement strategy to properly engage both internal and external stakeholders. Which of the following approaches should they follow?
explanation: The project manager and project team should develop and tailor appropriate management strategies for effectively engaging stakeholders in project decisions and execution. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 503 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 10

choices.text: Analyze all stakeholders and tailor an engagement strategy for each stakeholder.
Develop one engagement strategy for all stakeholders.
Arrange regular face to face meetings with both internal and external stakeholders.
Ask the project sponsor to take the responsibility of senior management engagement.

text: You are managing a mechanical project. The sponsor is worried about the status of the project. You provided him with the earned value calculations AC = $24,100, ETC = $44,600, VAC = -$1,600, BAC =$89,000, and EAC = $91,100. Your sponsor asks you to forecast how much money you expect to spend on the remainder of the project. Which is the best estimate to use for this forecast?
explanation: No need for calculations it asks about ETC. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 265 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: Mark compares his team velocity which is 40 story points per iteration to another team's velocity in the same organization with the same size working on similar project and found their velocity to be 55 story points per iteration. What should Mark do?
explanation: There is no meaningful comparison of velocity “between” different teams, since such teams may have different approaches to estimation. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Acquire more resources to his team.
Do nothing.
Bring this issue for discussion in the retrospective.
Discuss the issue in the next standup meeting.

text: Mark is managing a project to prepare a chemical compound. During project executing, a senior manager has requested from Mark the data sheet of one of the chemical components used to prepare the chemical compound. The senior manager is not authorized to see this data but he insists to see it. What should Mark do in this situation?
explanation: As the senior manager is not authorized to see this data, so the project manager must ensure compliance. He should meet the senior manager as soon as possible to discuss the matter further with him and know the exact reason for getting this data. Domain = Business Environment. The Standard for Project Management, Page 27

choices.text: Provide the needed data sheet to the senior manager.
Have a meeting with the senior manager to discuss this topic.
Escalate this topic to the project steering committee.
Ask the project sponsor to take the responsibility of senior management engagement.

text: You are managing a construction project. You are in the process of Monitor Risks. You are searching for new risks and making sure that implemented risk responses are effective. You find that a lower-priority risk has suddenly become more likely. Where do you record low-priority risks?
explanation: Low priority risks are recorded in watchlist. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 423

choices.text: Risk Register
The watchlist
Risk Management Plan
Risk Report

text: Which of the following describes the contents of a resources management plan?
explanation: Conflict resolution techniques, personnel performance appraisal and team performance assessment are not included in the resources management plan. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 318

choices.text: Guidance on how to acquire team and physical resources, project organization charts, conflict resolution techniques and release plan.
Competence, roles, personnel performance appraisal and recognition and rewards.
Identification of resources, release plan, staff training needs, and team performance assessment.
Authority, Training Needs, Recognition and Rewards, and Release Plan.

text: You have been assigned as a project manager for a large project to develop an accounting system using a predictive life cycle. The project is expected to be finished in 6 months and is budgeted at $100,000. During project execution, you discover that some requirements have not been included in the project scope. Failure to meet these requirements may result in legal actions from the customer of the project against your company. However, implementing these requirements may increase the project schedule and budget. What should you do?
explanation: All requirements must be met, so you can request a change to implement the change first, then as a result of the change, you will get the needed time and funds. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Submit a change request to add the missed requirements to the project scope.
Do nothing, these requirements are not a part of the project scope.
This is scope creep; these requirements must be ignored.
Request additional funds and time to implement these requirements.

text: While analyzing the last year performance of the company, it was found that the company has lost many customers especially who used the products of the company during last year. The CEO informed all the project managers to pay more attention to quality management. He focused not just on giving customers products without any defects by keeping the defective ones out of the customer hands, but keeping errors out of the process of producing deliverables. Keeping errors out of the process is known as:
explanation: Prevention means keeping errors out of the process and inspection means keeping errors out of the hands of the customer. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 274

choices.text: Variable sampling
Attribute sampling
Prevention
Inspection

text: Jim is managing a large industrial project. His project is halfway done, when he held a meeting with the project team and other stakeholders to identify new risks and reevaluate the current risks. This practice is known as:
explanation: Risk review is a meeting to analyze the status of existing risks and identify new risks. This includes determining if the risk is still active and if there have been changes to the risk attributes (such as probability, impact, urgency, etc.). Risk responses are evaluated to determine if they are effective or should be updated. New risks may be identified and analyzed and risks that are no longer active may be closed. Risk reassessment is an example of a risk-review meeting. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 180

choices.text: Risk audits
Risk reassessment
Risk identification
Developing risk responses

text: During project execution which adopts a predictive life cycle, the customer has requested a change to the project scope which may affect other constraints. The project manager called for a meeting for the project team to discuss how to make this change. This is an example of:
explanation: This situation indicates a lack of a change management plan which describes the procedures required to make a change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 88.

choices.text: Lack of a clear project scope statement.
Lack of a change management plan.
Lack of a communications management plan.
Good team relations.

text: Luis is assigned as a project manager for a new telecommunication network to swap the old mobile phone network in a city to a new network. He is collecting everything to initiate the new project. He collects historical information, high level requirements from key stakeholders, what is included in the project, high level schedule and high level budget. Which of the following should not be taken into consideration during this stage of the project?
explanation: Rough order of magnitude estimates, Constraints, assumptions, and Initial project organization are all determined in project initiating. What to purchase or make or buy decisions occur in the Plan Procurement Management process during planning process group. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 77 and 468 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 46

choices.text: What to purchase
Rough order of magnitude estimates
Constraints and assumptions
Initial project organization

text: You are the project manager of an industrial design project that has teams distributed in four countries Spain, India, France and Germany. You want all team members get the right message, so you translate the project plan into Spanish, Hindi, French, and German. Which of the following best describes what you do when you have your communications translated?
explanation: Encoding means that thoughts or ideas are translated (encoded) into language by the sender. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 371

choices.text: Encoding
Decoding
Feedback
Effective listening

text: A RACI chart is an example of:
explanation: A RACI is a detailed responsibility assignment matrix. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 317 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 189

choices.text: Hierarchical-type organization chart
Text-oriented position description
Responsibility assignment matrix
Control Charts

text: Joseph is managing a large industrial project. He and his team decide to outsource the installation work to a more experienced company, because the installation work is very risky. Only one company has submitted a proposal. They organizational policies for selecting a seller states that there must be at least two potential sellers to start bidding process. Which technique should the project manager use to help him expand the potential sellers list?
explanation: Placing advertisements in publications like newspapers or specialty publications, can help in expanding list of potential sellers and it is a tool used during conducting procurement process. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 487

choices.text: Bidder conference
Procurement negotiations
Expert judgment
Advertising

text: An organization has adopted an agile approach recently for a new project. The project sponsor directs their project manager, who has been assigned as a team facilitator, to add a new high-priority feature to the product. How should the project manager handle this scope change?
explanation: It is the ultimate responsibility of the product owner to manage and prioritize the product backlog. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 52.

choices.text: The project manager should add the new feature to the product backlog and make it the top priority.
On an agile project, changes are welcomed at any time, so this feature should be assigned to the team for development.
Communicate the new feature request to the product owner to be considered in the next backlog session.
Tell the sponsor to talk with the product owner about this feature, reminding them that the product owner has authority to prioritize features.

text: Which of the following statements best describes when risk identification is performed during the project?
explanation: Risk identification process is performed throughout the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 409 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 122

choices.text: During project planning and project monitoring and controlling.
Whenever there is a change to the project.
During project planning.
Throughout the project.

text: A project manager is assigned to new project chartered to build a bridge between two islands. This type of project has never been done before in his organization. The project manager has decided to build a basic project schedule and to detail the work of each part as the current part nears completion. What best describes what the project manager is doing?
explanation: Progressive elaboration is the iterative process of increasing the level of detail in a project management plan as greater amounts of information and more accurate estimates become available. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 565

choices.text: The process of Define Scope
The process of progressive elaboration
The process of Integrated Change Control
The process of controlling the project by taking corrective action when necessary

text: Which of the following is the top priority for an agile project?
explanation: As per the agile manifesto "Our highest priority is to satisfy the customer through early and continuous delivery of valuable software". So the top priority is satisfying the customer through delivery of valuable product. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 8

choices.text: Working collaboratively with customer and having a lightweight contract.
Satisfying the customer through delivery of valuable product.
Providing working software and accurate documentation.
Responding to change and following adaptive plan.

text: After the completion of the first iteration, the product owner holds a meeting with the team. The product owner moves some features to top of the product backlog to be added to the next iteration, because those features will provide higher value for the customer. This is closely related to which Agile Manifesto value?
explanation: The backlog refinement “grooming the backlog” means that everyone involved in the project gathers to discuss and update the items in the backlog. It is more closely related to the Agile Manifesto value of "Responding to change over following a plan" as items in the backlog can change in priority according to stakeholders request, there is no fixed plan like traditional projects that must be followed and changed only through change requests Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 97

choices.text: Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
Working software over comprehensive documentation
Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Responding to change over following a plan

text: During an iteration review, after the team has demonstrated the product increment, the project stakeholders have expressed their concern about the product increment being demonstrated as it presents some low priority features and it does not represent their expectations. What is most likely the cause of this situation?
explanation: The product owner should maintain the product backlog so that it represents the customer needs. As the product increment contains low priority items, so this means that the product backlog has not been maintained and prioritized to reflect the customer needs. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 52

choices.text: The product owner did not properly prioritize the product backlog.
The product owner has not properly defined the acceptance criteria.
The team has developed a minimal viable product.
The team has created a prototype.

text: Which of the following documents are the main risk management document where most of the risk information is kept, which are developed along the risk management processes from identify risks through perform qualitative and quantitative risk analysis to plan risk responses, implement risk responses and monitor risks?
explanation: The risk register and risk report are the main risk documents that contain most of risk information. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 396 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 185 and 190

choices.text: Watchlist and Risk Breakdown Structure
Risk Register and Risk Report
Risk Response Plan and Risk Report
Risk Management Plan and Risk Response Plan

text: If a risk event has a 80% chance of occurring, and the consequences will be a $100,000 loss, what does $80,000 represent?
explanation: Expected monetary value is calculated by multiplying the probability and impact of a risk event. EMV = P * I = 100000 * 0.8 = 80000 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 435 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 176

choices.text: Present value
Contingency budget
Risk value
Expected monetary value

text: During a major construction project in a predictive environment, a team member came to the project manager with a change request. The change has a very good idea and will be useful to the project. The project manager asked him to write a report describing the benefits of the change. What is the most appropriate advice for this situation?
explanation: The cost-benefit analysis done during project planning is not related to the change. The project sponsor doesn't approve change requests. What is meant by cost-benefit analysis here is the assumptions which the team member should provide when he requires the change. So the team member should do what was asked so that the project manager can make a decision based on the information provided. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: He should remind the project manager that the cost-benefit analysis for the project was done during project planning.
The project manager should do this report himself as a part of his job.
The team member should do what was asked because this sort of information must be given to the project sponsor to make the change.
A project manager must be able to weigh the benefits of the change versus the costs and compare them to other possible changes. The team member should do what was asked.

text: George is the project manager for a large software project. He has impleemnetd an automated reporting system, so that all stakeholders can get their needed reports automatically at any time using the electronic reporting system. Stakeholders don't need to wait for any report to be sent. Later, he was contacted by Peter, a senior manager, who has asked him to send him the project status report using a template which has been used before implementing the new system. What should George do in this situation?
explanation: The senior manager may find his needed information in the new system, but he finds some difficulties that is why he requested from the project manager to send him the status report in the old format. The best solution is to contact the senior manager and know his requirements in details. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 367 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: Modify the reporting system to include the project status report with the old template.
Send him the needed report with the needed template.
Contact Peter and ask him about his needs in details.
Grant Peter access to the new reporting system.

text: You are managing a product development project. Your customer tells you that you need to plan to have an audit team start auditing so that it finishes just before documentation begins. But other than that, she says it can start as late in the project as necessary. What’s the relationship between the auditing activity and the documentation activity?
explanation: The first activity (auditing) should be finished totally before the next activity (documentation) begins, so the relationship is finish-to-start (FS). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 190 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 59

choices.text: Start-to-Start (SS)
Start-to-Finish (SF)
Finish-to-Start (FS)
Finish-to-Finish (FF)

text: Andrew is an accountant who has recently joined XYZ organization which has a long agile approach. Andrew has a good experience in traditional projects, but he has never worked on agile projects before. He asks an agile coach how costs and returns are monitored and tracked on agile projects. Which of the following is a correct answer to Andrew's question?
explanation: Agile approach follows the principle of incremental delivery which means that each release will deliver value. So, ROI can be generated early as soon as value is delivered. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 16

choices.text: It is the same as traditional projects as both use S-curve.
It is the same as traditional projects as both use earned value management.
Cost is not a concern as it is fixed. In agile projects scope is variable.
ROI can be generated while executing the project.

text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto values are closely related to work in progress (WIP)?
explanation: Work in progress (WIP), is the term given to work that has been started but has not yet been completed. Excessive levels of WIP are associated with a number of problems like hiding bottlenecks, representing risk in the form of potential rework, and consuming investment capital, and delivering no return. The second principle of Agile Manifesto states that we value working software over comprehensive documentation, so it is closely related to WIP. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 25

choices.text: Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
Working software over comprehensive documentation
Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Responding to change over following a plan

text: Which of the following represents a win-win negotiation?
explanation: The buyer gets the work completed and the seller gets a reasonable profit is an example of win-win negotiation. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 167

choices.text: Fine, we agree on the price you asked for as you will provide the service we need with all mentioned specifications.
We will go with Sam's suggestion, all others are rejected.
Let's accept the less profit for now, this will give us an opportunity to penetrate this new market.
We will implement the first part of the approach proposed by Mike and the second part of the approach proposed by Julia.

text: You are a project manager for a large tele-communication project. The project has 1250 stakeholders. You feel that misunderstandings can occur in your project because the project is a large one and contains a large number of stakeholders. You need to avoid these misunderstandings. What can you use to reduce the number of such misunderstandings?
explanation: Influence and delegating are leadership tools. Collaboration and compromise are conflict resolution techniques. Weekly meeting with 1250 stakeholders will not be effective. But common understanding and communications management can reduce misunderstanding. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 362

choices.text: Common understanding and communications management
A weekly meeting for all stakeholders
Collaboration and compromise
Influence and delegating

text: Due to external factors, a project has been terminated early. The project manager sends the final project report to project stakeholders when a functional manager requests a copy of the report. What should the project manager do in this situation?
explanation: The communications management plan contains which information should be sent to which stakeholder. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 377 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 186

choices.text: Ask the functional manager why he needs the report.
Don't send the report and inform his manager.
Send the report.
Consult the communication management plan.

text: Julian is managing a large construction project with a total budget of $450,000. According to the schedule, his team should have completed 45% of the work by now. But at the latest status report, the team only reported that 40% of the work has actually been completed. The team has spent $165,000 till now on the project. How would he best describe the status of the project?
explanation: EV=180000, PV=202500, AC=165000 EV < PV, EV > AC Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 261 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 100

choices.text: The project is ahead of schedule and within its budget.
The project is behind schedule and within its budget.
The project is ahead of schedule and over its budget.
The project is behind schedule and over its budget.

text: You are managing a software project in an agile development environment. After the first round of risk identification, a team member who has never worked in agile environment asks you when the qualitative risk analysis is performed. What would be your answer?
explanation: In an agile development environment, the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process is conducted before the start of each iteration. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 421

choices.text: During project planning.
Only before the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process.
After the first round of risk identification.
Before the start of each iteration.

text: An advantage and a disadvantage of using checklists in risk identification:
explanation: While a checklist may be quick and simple to use, it is impossible to build an exhaustive one, and care should be taken to ensure the checklist is not used to avoid the effort of proper risk identification. The project team should also explore items that do not appear on the checklist. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 414

choices.text: Simple to use and can't be used alone.
Easy to calculate probability and impact and quiet people may not contribute.
Thinking outside the box and hard to use.
Helps understand unfamiliar parts of the project and takes too much time.

text: Scott is the project manager for a large and complex software project in his organization. The organization has only one web developer and he is assigned full time to this project. Maria is the IT manager, she needs to assign some functional work to the same resource. Maria is classified in this project according to the direction of influence as:
explanation: Sideward stakeholders are the peers of the project manager, such as other project managers or middle managers who are in competition for scarce project resources or who collaborate with the project manager in sharing resources or information. Domain = People. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 513

choices.text: Upward
Downward
Outward
Sideward

text: Robert is managing a large manufacturing project. Dave is the project sponsor and at the same time he is the direct manager of Robert. Dave wants to know about the earned value calculations. Robert provided him with this data the project total budget is 120,000$, the total amount spent till now is 70,000$ and the team has actually completed 50% worth of work. If the future project work will be accomplished at the planned rate, what should the Estimate at Completion (EAC) be?
explanation: As the future work will be accomplished at the planned rate, so EAC = AC + BAC - EV. EV = 0.5 * 120,000, EV = 60,000$. BAC = 120,000$ and AC = 70,000$. EAC = 70,000+120,000-60,000 = 130,000$. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 265 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 104

text: Both teams A and B after the first few iterations have determined their velocity to be 50 story points per iteration. Team A has committed to the planned velocity for the remaining iterations of the project. Team B has constantly exceeded the planned velocity. Which team has higher efficiency?
explanation: The team who commits to the planned velocity has higher efficiency. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide, Page 55

choices.text: Team A
Team B
Both have the same efficiency
Not enough information

text: Which of the following projects needs to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly than other projects?
explanation: Generally projects that subject to various elements of ambiguity and change like projects that adopt agile life cycles have an inherent need to communicate evolving and emerging details more frequently and quickly. In this question the IT project with not clear scope where the detailed scope is defined and approved before the start of each iteration adopts adaptive or agile life cycle. Other mentioned projects are all examples of projects with predictive life cycles. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 365

choices.text: An IT project with not clear scope, as the detailed scope is defined and approved before the start of each iteration.
A construction project where scope, time, and cost are determined in the early phases of the project life cycle.
A tele-communications project with predictive life cycle and has 550 stakeholders.
An industrial project where changes to the scope are carefully managed.

text: Linda is a project manager for a large tele-communication project. She is planning for risk management with her team. She holds a brainstorming session to discover and evaluate potential project risks. Which of the following methods can they use in prioritizing these risks?
explanation: Probability and impact matrix helps the team to focus on the top risks with high probability and impact. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 176

choices.text: Tornado Diagram
Watchlist
Simulation
Probability and impact matrix

text: During the Plan Risk Responses process, the project team is analyzing ways to deal with a top risk. A team member suggests to assign the activity to a more experienced team members. Another one suggests to do the risky activities at the beginning of the project or to ignore the risk. The project manager suggests to insure against the risky activity. A functional manager suggests to wait to see if the risk happens. Which of the following is a risk response strategy that the team has forgotten to do?
explanation: Risk avoidance is the only strategy which is not mentioned here. Removing the activity which causes the risk is risk avoidance. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 443 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 123

choices.text: Remove the activity which causes the risk from the project.
Provide more training to team members.
Outsource the activity.
Decrease probability or impact of the risk.

text: Midway through the project and after many clashes between the team members, it has been noticed that the team members started to adjust their work habits and behaviors and a sense of cohesion and unity has started to emerge between team members. At this stage, what is the most appropriate leadership style?
explanation: When the team reaches the norming phase, the members have successfully created rules (team norms) to help govern themselves. This doesn’t mean the team leader can simply go into cruise control mode, however. Instead, the leader needs to play a supporting role. The team will still need help with conflict resolution, as well as reminders to enforce the norms they have created.

choices.text: Directing
Supporting
Coaching
Delegating

text: You are a project manager for an industrial project. You are analyzing with the sponsor and project team members whether to do part of the project by your company or outsource this part to another company. Finally you all agree to do the work by your company because:
explanation: This is an example of make or buy analysis. You can decide to buy to transfer risks to the seller. And you can decide to make if the project contains proprietary data. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 473 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 65

choices.text: Your company has limited resources.
Your company doesn't want to have control over the work.
There is a lot of proprietary data.
Your company doesn't have experienced manpower.

text: During executing a large construction project, the customer required a change to a major work package. You evaluate the impact of the change on all the project constraints and tell the customer that the change will cost $10,000 and will add three more weeks to the project duration. But the customer says that he doesn’t have the time or budget to allow the change. What’s the best way for you to handle this situation?
explanation: You must first know the reasons for the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Have the project sponsor meet with the customer to explain the situation.
Hold a meeting with the customer to know why he required the change.
Do nothing.
Tell the customer that the added time and cost are much less than the benefits resulting from the change.

text: Who is responsible to ensure that organizational procurement policies are followed?
explanation: It is the project management team’s responsibility to make certain that all procurements meet the specific needs of the project while working with the procurement office to ensure organizational procurement policies are followed. Domain = Business Environment. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 461

choices.text: Project manager
Procurement office
Project management team
Both project management team and procurement office

text: Which of the following statements best describes when stakeholders identification is performed during the project?
explanation: Detailed stakeholder identification is a continuous activity throughout the project. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 11

choices.text: At the beginning of the project
Throughout the project
Only when a new stakeholder is discovered
Before starting to prepare the project management plan

text: You are a project manager for a large construction project to build a large shopping mall. All the design work will be outsourced to other companies. Your team developed the procurement statement of work (SOW). The procurement team send request for proposal to potential sellers, but the prices they offered are very high. You and the procurement manager agreed that there must be some negotiation with the potential sellers to get fair and reasonable price. Typically, who leads procurement negotiation?
explanation: The negotiation should be led by a member of the procurement team that has the authority to sign contracts. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 488

choices.text: Project manager
Procurement manager
Project sponsor
Both project manager and procurement manager

text: You are the project manager performing intranet project for your company. During planning the project with your team, you divide up all of the work into work packages and create a WBS. For each one of the work packages, you write down details such as initial estimates, assumptions, predecessor and successor work packages. Where do you store all of that information?
explanation: WBS dictionary gives all the details that you know about each work package in the WBS, including estimates and billing information. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 162 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 85

choices.text: Scope Management Plan
WBS
WBS Dictionary
Project Scope Statement

text: An agile team is involved in a network installation project using scrum methodology. A network migration activity must be done during downtime and a team member must be assigned to this task. None of the team members wants to sign up for this task. How should this situation be addressed?
explanation: Task assignments are done by the team as the team is self-managed and self-organized. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 44

choices.text: The senior most team member should be assigned to this task.
The scrum master should try first to reschedule the activity to daytime.
The scrum master should decide who will pick up this task.
The team should decide who will pick up this task.

text: After a great deal of effort, finally, the project manager has made a change to the scope baseline. What should he do next?
explanation: As the change is already made, this means that the impacts were evaluated, the change was submitted to CCB and approved. The remaining action is to update the project management plan and project documents to reflect the change. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 115 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 66.

choices.text: Ask the sponsor to approve the change.
Evaluate impacts on other constraints like time, cost, and resources.
Revise the project management plan and project documents.
Send the change request to the Change Control Board.

text: You are working in the Project Management Office (PMO) for a multinational organization. You were coaching new project managers on risk management. You stated that all agreements should be included during project risk identification with team members and stakeholders. One of the attendees asks why agreements need to be included during project risk identification, what would be your answer?
explanation: If the project requires external procurement of resources, the agreements may have information such as milestone dates, contract type, acceptance criteria, and awards and penalties that can present threats or opportunities. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 413

choices.text: Because agreements contain the project risks that are already transferred to seller.
Because agreements are made to transfer project risks to the seller.
Because agreements may have information such as milestone dates, contract type, acceptance criteria, and awards and penalties that can present threats or opportunities.
Not all agreements are included but contracts should be included because they are formal agreements.

text: What do you expect when you are submitting an invitation for bid to sellers?
explanation: A bid is a response for invitation for bid. Terms such as bid, tender, or quotation are generally used when the seller selection decision is based on price (as when buying commercial or standard items). Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 477 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 74–75

choices.text: Offers which will differ significantly in solution approach and price.
Offers which are similar enough to allow a selection based on price.
Offers which are not primarily written to win against competition.
General information on a seller’s ability to provide the goods or services.

text: During creating the quality management plan, two team members are arguing about which technique to use in testing the products of the project. One of them suggests that they should test only a sample of the product, because the population would be too much to be tested. The other team member objects to this opinion and suggests to test all the products because the quality should be as high as possible. If the project manager tells the team, "We will schedule this discussion for our team meeting next week", the project manager is:
explanation: Postpone solving the problem means withdrawal. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 169

choices.text: Compromising
Forcing
Withdrawing
Confronting

text: How should you plan for change management in a predictive environment?
explanation: Change is inevitable, so planning for change must be through a change management plan or a group of management plans where change management for each knowledge area can be performed separately. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 88.

choices.text: Changes are generally not predictable, therefore planning for change management cannot be reasonable.
Planning for change management should be done while the various change control processes are being applied.
Change management can be planned in a set of management plans or a specific change management plan.
Changes are a sign of poor planning. One should avoid changes during a project, thus eliminating the need to manage them.

text: What is the purpose of a risk workshop?
explanation: To undertake risk identification, the project team may conduct a specialized meeting (often called a risk workshop). Most risk workshops include some form of brainstorming, but other risk identification techniques may be included. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 416

choices.text: The project team may conduct risk workshops for reassessment of current risks, the closing of risks that are outdated.
The project team may conduct risk workshops to develop risk responses for identified project risks.
The project team may conduct risk workshops for risk identification.
The project team may conduct risk workshops to clarify the assessment of each risk’s importance to the project.

text: Project changes are approved during the _____  process and verified during the______ process.
explanation: Project changes are approved during the Perform Integrated Change Control process and verified during the Control Quality process. Domain = Process PMBOK 6th edition, Page 301

choices.text: Perform Integrated Change Control, Control Quality.
Perform Integrated Change Control, Validate Scope.
Perform Integrated Change Control, Control Scope.
Monitor and Control Project Work, Validate Scope..

text: Which of the following statements best describes how stakeholders are involved on a project?
explanation: The project manager is the one who creates the project schedule not the stakeholders during the Develop Schedule process. The project manager determines the resource needs for the project during the Estimate Activity Resources process. The project sponsor issues the project charter and approves it, not the stakeholders. So the best answer is that the stakeholders help the project manager determine the project constraints and product deliverables. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 14

choices.text: They help to determine project constraints and product deliverables.
They help determine the project deliverables and project schedule.
They help to provide project assumptions and determine the resources needs on the project.
They create the project management plan and approve the project charter.

text: The finance manager has contacted the project manager asking him that the billing process of the project has not been triggered yet. As per the organization policy, once the customer accepts the deliverables, the project should be closed on the organization internal tools and billing process starts. The project manager has responded that this project has been completed and he is now assigned to another project. Which of the following might be the root cause for this situation?
explanation: The activities necessary for the administrative closure of the project or phase include making certain that all documents and deliverables are up-to-date and that all issues are resolved; Confirming the delivery and formal acceptance of deliverables by the customer; Ensuring that all costs are charged to the project; Closing project accounts;..etc. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 123 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 47

choices.text: It was an agile project, so there is no project closure process.
The project is still in transition phase.
The project manager did not perform administrative project closure.
Triggering billing is a financial task, the financial manager should do it himself.

text: Which of the following is not a question to be asked during the daily standup?
explanation: During daily standup the scrum team discuss what did I complete since the last standup? What am I planning to complete between now and the next standup? And What are my impediments (or risks or problems)? An antipattern typically seen in standups is that the team begins to solve problems as they become apparent. Standups are for realizing there are problems—not for solving them. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 54

choices.text: Are there any roadblocks?
What do you plan to finish today?
What have you worked on since last meeting?
Are there any other issues need to be discussed before we finish the daily standup?

text: The first principle of the Agile Manifesto states that: "Our highest priority is to satisfy the customer through early and continuous delivery of valuable software". How can the team ensure that the features or deliverables they create are valuable for the customer?
explanation: The product owner continuously maintain and refine the product backlog and prioritizes the features with the highest value to the customer to be on the top of the backlog. Domain = Business Environment. Agile Practice Guide, Page 52

choices.text: By creating and prioritizing the product backlog by the product owner.
By having daily standups to review what has been achieved so far.
By continuously communicating with the customer throughout the iteration.
By creating Kanban board and working on the high value features first.

text: Adam has been assigned as a project manager for a software project as a replacement for another project manager. The project team consists of 12 members with different skills and experience and each has an action plan for personal development. He wants to adapt his leadership style to support team members according to their action plans for personal development. Which of the following models can help him in this situation?
explanation: The OSCAR coaching and mentoring model helps individuals adapt their coaching or leadership styles to support individuals who have an action plan for personal development. The model refers to five contributing factors outcome, situation, choices/consequences, actions and review. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 156

choices.text: ADKAR
OSCAR
Virginia Satir
Cynefin Framework

text: Which of the following Agile Manifesto principles is closely related to backlog refinement?
explanation: As expressed in Agile Manifesto Principle 2, staying flexible to meet the customer’s changing business needs is fundamental to agile, and the backlog is one of the key ways that agile teams ensure this adaptability. The backlog is always evolving, and it needs to be kept continually updated with the latest requirements and new information. Domain = Process. Agile Practice Guide Page 8 and 52.

choices.text: Business people and developers must work together daily throughout the project.
Continuous attention to technical excellence and good design enhances agility.
Welcome changing requirements, even late in development. Agile processes harness change for the customer’s competitive advantage.
Agile processes promote sustainable development. The sponsors, developers, and users should be able to maintain a constant pace indefinitely.

text: All of the following are examples of non-verbal communication except:
explanation: Examples of nonverbal communication include appropriate body language to transmit meaning through gestures, tone of voice, and facial expressions. Mirroring and eye contact are also important techniques. Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 384 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 13

choices.text: A project manager is waving his hands to a team member to welcome him.
A trainer is looking at the eyes of his trainees to know if they understand what he said or not.
A team leader advising a team member about how to use non-verbal communication.
A team leader is using an angry look to a team member who came to work late.

text: A project has been initiated in ABC organization to develop a new HR system for a customer. The project team will adopt an adaptive approach for this project. The project team wants to clarify and organize the customer requirements for the new system. The project team is working with the customer to clarify all possible sequences of interactions between the system and the users the particular goals to achieve. Which of the following artifacts can the project team and customer use in this situation?
explanation: Use case describes and explores how a user interacts with a system to achieve a specific goal. Domain = Process. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 190

choices.text: Throughput chart
User story
Use case
Story map

text: Rebecca is a scrum master working on a large software project. The product owner assigned to the project is always very busy, as he has some other work assigned to him within the organization. As a result, the product owner spends very little time with the team which causes a lot of troubles. What should Rebecca do?
explanation: The product owner needs to participate in scrum events as he is a customer representative. He must be involved to ensure the project success. Domain = People. Agile Practice Guide Page 41

choices.text: Coach the product owner on the importance of participating in scrum events.
Request another product owner and distribute the work between them.
Escalate the product owner to the project sponsor.
Take the product owner responsibilities during his absence.

text: XYZ organization executives have assigned Harry to form a project team to deliver a software to a client. Harry has formed the project team and motivated them towards achieving the project goal and influenced them to align their individual interests in favor of collective effort, and achieve success as a project team rather than as individuals. The organization used ______ while Harry used ______.
explanation: The organization exercised authority by assigning Harry to form the team, while Harry demonstrated leadership by motivating and influencing the team to align their interests toward collective success. Domain = People.

choices.text: Stewardship, responsibility
Responsibility, stewardship
Authority, leadership
Leadership, authority

text: Pierre is managing a large software project which involves many stakeholders. His organization had a high turnover rate during the last year. To avoid the risks resulting from high turnover rate, he needs to measure the team members satisfaction. He decided to use mood chart. He informed the project team that at the end of each day, project team members can use emojis to indicate their frame of mind. What is a disadvantage of using mood chart that might affect these measures?
explanation: A mood chart can track the mood or reactions of a group of very important stakeholders—the project team. At the end of each day, project team members can use colors, numbers, or emojis to indicate their frame of mind. Tracking the project team’s mood or individual project team member’s moods can help to identify potential issues and areas for improvement to reduce turnover rate. Mood charts can be subjective. Domain = People. PMBOK 7th edition, Page 103

choices.text: Mood charts will not help to reduce turnover rate.
Mood charts will not help to identify potential team issues.
Mood charts does not measure the project team morale.
Mood charts can be subjective.

text: Adam is a project manager for an electronic board design project. The project has a 50% chance of making cost saving for the company $215,000 over the next year. It has a 30% chance of costing the company $120,000. What's the project’s expected monetary value?
explanation: $215,000 x 0.5 = $ 107,500 savings, and $120,000 x 0.30 = - $36,000 expenses. Add them together and you get $71,500 Domain = Process. PMBOK 6th edition, Page 435 and PMBOK 7th edition, Page 176
